code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a fluent, single‑sentence description."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to turn a numeric draft pick into an ordinal string.
    def ordinal(n: str) -> str:
        # Strip possible quotes and whitespace.
        n = n.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
        try:
            i = int(n)
        except ValueError:
            return n
        suffix = "th"
        if 10 <= i % 100 <= 20:
            suffix = "th"
        else:
            if i % 10 == 1:
                suffix = "st"
            elif i % 10 == 2:
                suffix = "nd"
            elif i % 10 == 3:
                suffix = "rd"
        return f"{i}{suffix}"

    # Helper to format ISO dates (YYYY-MM-DD) into a readable form e.g., "2 September 1995".
    def format_date(d: str) -> str:
        import datetime

        d = d.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
        try:
            dt = datetime.datetime.strptime(d, "%Y-%m-%d")
            return dt.strftime("%-d %B %Y")  # e.g., "2 September 1995"
        except ValueError:
            # If parsing fails, return the original string.
            return d

    # Predicate → fragment generator (subject omitted, used later when grouping).
    # Updated fragment templates accept both subject and object to allow possessive constructions.
    fragment_templates = {
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",
        "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o} metres tall",
        "weight": lambda s, o: f"{s} weighs {o}",
        "position": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays as a {o}",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s club is {o}",
        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s current club is {o}",
        "formerTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} formerly played for {o}",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} competes in the {o}",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s manager is {o}",
        "coach": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s coach is {o}",
        "draftYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in {o}",
        "draftRound": lambda s, o: f"{s} was selected in round {o}",
        "draftPick": lambda s, o: f"{s} was the {ordinal(o)} pick",
        "playerNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} wears number {o}",
        "currentteam": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} debuted for {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{s} has leader {o}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s home ground is {o}",
        "stadium": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s stadium is {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
        "generalManager": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s general manager is {o}",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s chairman is {o}",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"{s} participated in the {o} season",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s currency is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s demonym is {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s official language is {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"{o} is spoken in {s}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{s} includes ethnic group {o}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s anthem is {o}",
        "mostChampions": lambda s, o: f"{s} most champions are {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s leader title is {o}",
        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s UTC offset is {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s time zone is {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s total area is {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded on {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to avoid repeating the subject.
    grouped = {}
    for t in triples:
        grouped.setdefault(t.subject, []).append((t.predicate, t.object))

    sentences = []
    for subject, preds in grouped.items():
        # Aggregate objects for each predicate to allow natural joining of multiple values.
        pred_objects = {}
        for pred, obj in preds:
            pred_objects.setdefault(pred, []).append(obj)

        fragments = []
        for pred, objs in pred_objects.items():
            # Join multiple objects with commas and 'and' for the last one.
            if len(objs) == 1:
                obj_str = objs[0]
            else:
                obj_str = ", ".join(objs[:-1]) + f" and {objs[-1]}"
            gen = fragment_templates.get(pred)
            if gen:
                # Pass both subject and object to the template.
                fragments.append(gen(subject, obj_str))
            else:
                fragments.append(f"{subject} {pred} is {obj_str}")

        # Build a sentence for this subject.
        if fragments:
            if len(fragments) == 1:
                sentence = f"{subject} {fragments[0]}."
            else:
                # Join fragments with commas, add 'and' before the last.
                body = ", ".join(fragments[:-1]) + f", and {fragments[-1]}"
                sentence = f"{subject} {body}."
            sentences.append(sentence)

    # Combine all subject sentences into one paragraph.
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
